Plan your lines carefully - once the sugar runs out, it's game over! Sugar, Sugar

Guide falling sugar particles into cups by drawing lines to redirect their flow. Each level requires filling all cups to their target amounts. Sugar flows continuously from the top, and your drawn lines act as physical barriers. Watch out for color filters that change sugar colors, and match the colored sugar to the correct cups. Plan your lines carefully - once the sugar runs out, it's game over!

Controls: Arrow Keys: Move cursor | Space: Place/remove line segment | Shift: Clear all lines | Enter: Start game | Esc: Pause/Unpause | R: Restart level

TEST_4.test_description: Testing color filter mechanics that transform sugar particles. Verifies that particles passing through color filters change to the appropriate color and that colored cups only accept matching colored sugar.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Draws lines guiding sugar through color filters before directing it to colored cups. Tests both correct color matching (should fill cup) and incorrect matching (should not count). Monitors particle color changes and cup acceptance logic.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Particles should visually change color when passing through filters. Colored cups should only increment fill amount for matching colored particles. Mismatched particles should not contribute to cup filling. Test succeeds when color matching works correctly.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game state management. Verifies that pausing stops all physics simulation, particle generation, and game logic while maintaining the current state for resumption.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Starts gameplay, draws some lines, waits for sugar to flow, then triggers pause. Monitors that all movement ceases. Unpauses and verifies gameplay resumes correctly from the paused state.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: When paused, all particles should freeze in place, no new particles should spawn, and physics should halt. Upon unpause, everything should resume smoothly. UI should clearly indicate paused state. Test succeeds when pause/unpause works without glitches.
